aidebug.summary: Inspect or write AITermux aidebug diagnostics
aidebug.detail: Inspect or write AITermux aidebug diagnostics. Use action=status to list motd/zshrc/bootstrap/projectling logs and metadata, action=health to score each runtime/debug chain, action=read to read a bounded head/tail/slice from an aidebug file, and action=event to append a diagnostic note. projectying is excluded because it has its own Aidebug chain.

apply_patch.summary: Create or edit files in the current working directory
apply_patch.detail: Create or edit files in the current working directory. DeepSeek should prefer the structured form: operation + target_file + content/find/replace, or edits[] for multiple small operations. target_file must be relative to the current cwd; do not use ~/,$HOME, or absolute paths. Use operation=write for full-file create/replace, operation=replace for exact text replacement, append/prepend/insert_before/insert_after for simple insertions, and delete for text or file deletion. Unified diff and *** Begin Patch remain available for advanced exact-context patches. Use this for code edits instead of shell heredocs, python writes, sed -i, tee, or ad-hoc file rewriting.

command.summary: Run one local shell command in the current Termux working directory
command.detail: Run one local shell command in the current Termux working directory. Supports shell commands, adb TCP workflows, and termux-api commands. Use command for inspection, build, tests, adb install/verify, and other runtime checks. Do not use it to create, move, copy, delete, or rewrite project files; apply_patch handles file creation and automatically creates parent directories. Clearly catastrophic commands create a pending confirmation and require the human to type yes before execution; blocked commands are not executed.

context.summary: Set the smart context budget for the next model request
context.detail: Set the smart context budget for the next model request. Use a percent from 0 to 100 to control how much of the shared entries context is returned next round: lower values save tokens for simple checks, higher values restore more memory for cross-file or long-running work. Low visibility is not memory loss: hidden context remains stored and can be restored later with a higher percent, including 100. This does not rewrite memory; contextmanage handles entry-id replacement and folding.

context_manage.summary: Manage projectling's shared entries context when the system says context is large
context_manage.detail: Manage projectling's shared entries context when the system says context is large. mode=status/list inspects entries, mode=replace replaces an entry id or id range with a compact summary, mode=fold folds old tool entries. Legacy mode=full/half/fold_tools is rejected because persona txt is no longer the active context store. Use target=both by default. target=role or target=persona points at the active persona board; legacy shared/public/dualstar aliases are accepted for compatibility but no longer represent separate shared memory. target=liaison can still be used only when a separate liaison role path is explicitly available in the current session.

contextmanage.detail: New ProjectLing context governance entry. Use it for entry-id status/list/replace/fold flows; legacy full/half/fold_tools compaction is rejected.

memory_add.summary: Write or append a permanent diary memory into projectling/memory/memory
memory_add.detail: Write or append a permanent diary memory into projectling/memory/memory.db. Use this when datememory.json exceeds the threshold or when the user asks to store long-term memory. Diary must be dated YYYY-MM-DD and include at least five keywords.

memory_check.summary: Search permanent diary memory by at least five keywords
memory_check.detail: Search permanent diary memory by at least five keywords. If the best hit rate is 80% or higher, returns the best diary detail; otherwise returns only date summaries.

memory_read.summary: Read permanent diary memory by exact dates only
memory_read.detail: Read permanent diary memory by exact dates only. Dates must be YYYY-MM-DD; do not use fuzzy ranges or months.

memory_status.summary: Inspect projectling permanent memory paths, datememory size, and SQLite diary counts
memory_status.detail: Inspect projectling permanent memory paths, datememory size, and SQLite diary counts.

terminal.summary: Create or control a collaborative Termux terminal backed by tmux
terminal.detail: Create or control a collaborative Termux terminal backed by tmux. Use action=start for long-running or interactive workflows: it opens a new Termux foreground session, logs terminal output under aidebug/projectling/terminal output, and returns log path/line/size metadata. Use action=send to send another command to the same terminal, action=info to inspect log metadata, and action=stop or action=close to end the tmux session. Read large logs with command using sed/head/tail slices instead of copying the whole log. Assistant-sent command strings must be clearly safe; risky or blocked commands are rejected here and should go through command so the human confirmation gate can handle them.

tool_manage.summary: Inspect and manage the tool box layer
tool_manage.detail: Inspect and manage the tool box layer. Use action=list to view summaries and visibility state, action=inspect to reveal selected tool details, action=expand/collapse to change a tool's schema visibility, and action=reset to restore all tools. tool_manage itself stays pinned and always visible.

update_plan.summary: Shared visible task plan for ProjectLing
update_plan.detail: Shared visible task plan for ProjectLing. Use mode=todo for medium multi-step tasks and mode=plan for complex phased work. Call action=start to create a plan, action=update whenever a step starts, finishes, changes, or blocks, action=complete for the final step, status to inspect, and reset to clear. Every start/update/complete triggers planner review before execution continues.

web_search.summary: Search the web through the configured Volcengine/Feedcoop WebSearch API
web_search.detail: Search the web through the configured Volcengine/Feedcoop WebSearch API. Use mode=summary when the user needs a fast synthesized answer with sources, mode=web when raw result lists are better, and mode=auto to prefer summary when available. Results are bounded for token control.
